key_change: All three key sources are EIA/Statista with heavy repetition across sessions and no diversity of data on closure dynamic

challenge_points.description: All three key sources are EIA/Statista with heavy repetition across sessions and no diversity of data on closure dynamics or Iranian conditions.
Claim that research parameters were optimized for a different project rests only on session annotations without direct evidence of the actual queries used.
Analysis does not examine whether partial or full closure scenarios alter the endurance calculation in the core hypothesis.

first_pass_summary: The research sessions established the Strait of Hormuz's critical importance to global oil transit but did not directly investigate the core hypothesis about Iran's capacity to endure a prolonged closure. The three sessions consistently returned sources confirming the Strait's role as a chokepoint for global energy security, with the EIA source ("World oil transit chokepoints critical to global energy security") and Statista chart on global oil shipments appearing across all sessions.
